# SPEC: #67 — feat: create /ship L2 recipe for autonomous branch-to-main delivery
<!-- Generated by start-feature-planning | Plan sub-agent output -->

**Intent:** Create the first L2 recipe in Meridian (`/ship`) that autonomously delivers a feature branch to main — committing uncommitted work, creating a PR, reviewing it, merging, and returning to main — with zero human approvals unless a genuine blocker is detected.

**Constraints:**
- C1: Current branch is NOT main, master, or develop (hard halt)
- C2: Git repository exists with a remote configured (hard halt)
- C3: Issue number extractable from branch name — NWWI (hard halt)
- C4: Orchestrator MUST delegate domain tasks to agents, never execute git/gh directly
- C5: Maximum 5 distinct agents (L2 limit); recipe uses 2 (repo-orchestrator, project-orchestrator)
- C6: Guardian logic is INLINE in the L2 SKILL.md — no dependency on workflow-guardian agent
- C7: Self-resolution MUST NOT change code or documentation; CAN retry operations, create issues, adjust PR metadata, rebase, delete/recreate branches
- C8: Evidence written to `.meridian/{issue}/evidence/ship/{YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS}.md`
- C9: Auto-approve is default; halt only on genuine blockers (merge conflicts, CI failures, protected branch violations, blocker-severity review findings)
- C10: Guardian evaluates inline after each step; does NOT ask user unless a blocker is detected
- C11: Skip steps whose preconditions are already satisfied (no uncommitted changes → skip commit; PR exists → skip create-pr)

**Failure Conditions:**
- F1: Current branch is a protected branch — hard halt (pre-flight)
- F2: No git remote configured — hard halt (pre-flight)
- F3: No issue number extractable from branch name — hard halt (pre-flight)
- F4: Merge conflicts that cannot be auto-resolved — halt, create tracking issue
- F5: PR review finds blocker-severity quality items — halt, present to user
- F6: Merge fails after max retry attempts — halt, create tracking issue
- F7: User explicitly cancels at a guardian-halted checkpoint (Vanish)

---

## Summary

The `/ship` recipe is the first L2 recipe in Meridian. It chains two existing L1 recipes (`commit-code`, `create-pr`) with three inline agent delegations to `repo-orchestrator` (review, merge, return-to-main). The guardian is implemented as inline decision logic within the SKILL.md orchestrator — not as a separate agent — which deviates from ADR 003's `workflow-guardian` design but is explicitly allowed by IDD constraint C6 of this recipe.

The most significant architectural risks identified through codebase analysis:
1. **`repo-orchestrator` has no merge capability** — its Bash usage rules allow only read-only operations. Steps 4 (merge) and 5 (return-to-main) both require write Bash commands (`gh pr merge`, `git checkout`, `git pull`) outside the agent's current documented scope. Resolution: expand repo-orchestrator's allowed Bash scope with named write exceptions.
2. **L1 checkpoint bypass will not work without L1 modifications** — commit-code has conditional auto-approve logic, but create-pr has `C5: Always checkpoint before PR creation`. Passing `ship_context.auto_approve: true` will not suppress L1 checkpoints without code changes. Resolution: modify both L1 recipes to check for `ship_context.auto_approve` and bypass their checkpoints when set.

---

## Affected Files

| File | Role | Change Needed |
|------|------|---------------|
| `core/components/recipes/ship/SKILL.md` | New L2 recipe execution blueprint | Create new |
| `core/components/recipes/ship/reference/intent.yaml` | Operational contract (IDD three elements) | Create new |
| `core/components/recipes/ship/templates/checkpoint.md` | Checkpoint artifact template (ADR 008 schema) | Create new |
| `core/components/recipes/ship/templates/guardian-decision.md` | Guardian auto-approve/halt decision record | Create new |
| `core/components/recipes/ship/templates/final-report.md` | Consolidated delivery evidence report | Create new |
| `core/components/agents/repo-orchestrator.md` | Agent capabilities — Bash scope | Modify: add merge/checkout as named write exceptions |
| `core/components/recipes/commit-code/SKILL.md` | Commit L1 recipe | Modify: add ship_context.auto_approve bypass in Step 3 |
| `core/components/recipes/create-pr/SKILL.md` | PR creation L1 recipe | Modify: add ship_context.auto_approve bypass in Step 2 (quality-conditional) |
| `docs/components/recipes.md` | Recipe catalog | Modify: add ship recipe entry |

---

## Technical Approach

**Strategy:** Create the `/ship` L2 recipe following the established SKILL.md + intent.yaml + templates pattern from `create-pr` (the golden standard). Guardian logic is inline in the SKILL.md orchestrator. The two critical gaps — merge capability in `repo-orchestrator` and L1 checkpoint bypass — are resolved during implementation: expand repo-orchestrator's Bash scope with named write exceptions; modify L1 recipes to check for `ship_context.auto_approve` and bypass checkpoints when set.

**Alternatives Considered:**

| Approach | Reason Rejected |
|----------|----------------|
| Invoke L1 and rely on its own auto-approve logic | create-pr has C5: always checkpoint — will halt regardless of quality. Breaks zero-friction intent. |
| Create new inline steps instead of chaining L1s | Duplicates logic, undermines L1 reuse value. L1s are designed to be composable. |
| Create workflow-guardian agent per ADR 003 | Agent doesn't exist, creating it is out of scope. Inline guardian is simpler and sufficient. |
| Expand repo-orchestrator Bash permissions globally | Named write exceptions with rationale is safer than global expansion. |

**Risks:**

| Risk | Mitigation | Severity |
|------|-----------|---------|
| `repo-orchestrator` has no merge skill; Bash is read-only | Expand Bash scope with `gh pr merge` and `git checkout {base} && git pull` as named write exceptions with rationale | High |
| `create-pr` C5 mandates checkpoint always — cannot suppress without L1 modification | Modify commit-code and create-pr to check `ship_context.auto_approve`; bypass only when quality is clean | High |
| ADR 003 specifies workflow-guardian agent; inline guardian deviates | Document deviation explicitly in SKILL.md with rationale; recommend ADR update as follow-on | Medium |
| Guardian auto-approve may pass PR with FAIL checklist items | Guardian evaluates `must_have_fail == 0` before auto-approving; any FAIL triggers halt | Medium |
| Squash merge loses individual commit history | Intentional for clean main history; documented as trade-off | Low |
